message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker and add readiness resolver E2E test

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Updated issue tracker state after DAG readiness resolver and transport
  policy convenience method additions

tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_2_1.sh:
- E2E scenario script for the bead readiness resolver (ft-1i2ge.2.1)
- Validates DAG-aware readiness resolution including cycle detection,
  missing dependency handling, and correct ready/blocked/done classification
- Follows project E2E conventions: structured JSONL logging, correlation
  IDs, timestamped artifact directories

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1i2ge","title":"[mission][epic] Live Beads-native Swarm Autopilot (ft mission)","description":"Build a live, graph-aware mission control loop inside FrankenTerm that continuously converts Beads state + live agent runtime state into safe, high-leverage next actions.

This is not an offline replay system. This is an online operations layer that decides what to do next now, dispatches work, and adapts based on outcomes.

Core objective:
- reduce swarm coordination overhead
- increase throughput on unblocked, high-impact work
- reduce conflict, thrash, and unsafe actions

## Success Criteria
1. The complete epic scope is delivered across child beads with no unresolved critical blockers and no loss of declared capability.
2. Every child feature/task in this epic includes comprehensive unit tests plus deterministic integration/E2E scenarios.
3. The epic includes an end-to-end validation matrix covering happy paths, failure injection, recovery behavior, and sustained/soak execution where applicable.
4. Structured logging and artifact standards are enforced across epic deliverables (timestamp, correlation_id, scenario_id, decision path, reason_code/error_code) with triage-ready evidence bundles.
5. Verification demonstrates stable, repeatable outcomes across reruns and confirms policy/safety invariants for all touched subsystems.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. ft mission surface exists and can plan, dispatch, monitor, and adapt work in a live swarm.
2. Planner decisions are explainable and reproducible from recorded mission state snapshots.
3. Planner supports explicit objective profiles (`throughput_first`, `risk_min_first`, `fairness_first`, `recovery_first`) with bounded custom tuning.
4. Dispatch path is policy-gated and file-reservation aware before issuing actionable commands.
5. Mission runtime is crash-recoverable with deterministic journal-based restart semantics.
6. Operator can run in dry-run/shadow mode and compare expected vs actual outcomes.
7. Mission telemetry proves net accretive value against manual baseline (faster unblocking, lower coordination latency/conflicts, lower intervention load).
8. Production readiness includes certified operator takeover game-days and emergency-control evidence.
9. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests, executable e2e test scripts, and detailed structured logs/artifacts proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
10. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge.sh and detailed structured logs under tests/e2e/logs/ft_1i2ge_*.jsonl proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
11. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Strategic rationale:
- This directly compounds existing ft assets (robot API, patterns, policy, workflows, MCP, beads, agent mail) into an always-on execution optimizer.
- It addresses the largest practical pain in large swarms: human/manual orchestration tax.
- It should be built as a capability framework, not a one-off heuristic This preserves all safety/test intent while reducing artificial serialization: early planner/safety implementation can start sooner, but final planner/safety completion still requires A6 contract validation before integration/hardening promotion.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:47:06Z"},{"id":116,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Plan-space audit pass complete (2026-02-22): reviewed all 74 ft-1i2ge* beads for self-contained clarity, dependency health, and test/evidence rigor.\n\nChanges made in this pass:\n1. Hardened G1-G4 acceptance criteria to explicitly require comprehensive unit tests (not just unit tests/benchmarks) while preserving full scenario scope.\n2. Tightened logging contracts where missing by enforcing correlation_id + reason_code + error_code fields in structured artifacts for G4 and H10.\n3. Revalidated all beads with direct ○ ft-og6q6.2.4 · [replay][T1.4] Enforce redaction, sensitivity classification, and retention boundaries   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: jemanuel · Type: task
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22
Labels: e2e-tests, integration-tests, logging, privacy, quality-gate, redaction, security, storage, test-coverage, test-logging, user-impact

Apply redaction and sensitivity policies during artifact capture so replay bundles preserve determinism-relevant semantics without leaking secrets or violating retention constraints.

## Deliverables

### CaptureRedactor
- CaptureRedactor struct with configurable redaction rules
- Operates on RecorderEvent stream between capture and serialization
- Sensitivity tiers: T1 (standard, safe for CI), T2 (internal, team access), T3 (restricted, incident-only)
- Redaction modes: mask (replace with block chars), hash (one-way SHA-256), drop (remove field entirely)
- Token patterns: API keys (sk-*, AKIA*, ghp_*, xoxb-*), JWT tokens, connection strings, bearer tokens
- Custom patterns via redaction_rules.toml config file

### SensitivityClassifier
- Classifies each RecorderEvent field into sensitivity tier
- IngressText.content: scan for token patterns, T2/T3 if found, else T1
- EgressOutput.content: same scanning
- Detection.matched_text: inherit tier from source event
- PolicyDecision.context: always T2 minimum (may contain internal details)
- Classifier is deterministic: same input always gets same classification

### RetentionEnforcer
- Enforces per-tier retention limits at artifact write time
- T1: 90 days default, T2: 30 days, T3: 7 days (all configurable)
- Expired artifacts are tombstoned (header preserved, content replaced with REDACTED_EXPIRED)

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests (>=30)
- Each token pattern correctly detected
- Mask mode replaces with correct-length replacement string
- Hash mode produces deterministic SHA-256 replacement
- Drop mode removes field entirely, preserving event structure
- T1/T2/T3 classification for each RecorderEventPayload variant
- Sensitivity inheritance for Detection events
- Mixed-tier event: highest tier wins
- Redaction is idempotent
- Redacted events still pass schema validation
- Custom patterns from redaction_rules.toml loaded correctly
- Retention enforcer tombstones expired T3 artifacts
- Unicode content redaction preserves valid UTF-8 boundaries

### Proptest Properties (P-06 through P-08 from taxonomy)
- Redaction stability: redacting same event twice yields identical output
- Redacted events still deserialize and pass schema validation
- No original secret text appears in redacted output

### E2E Test Script: tests/e2e/test_replay_redaction.sh
- Scenario 1: Capture with known API key in pane output, verify masked in .ftreplay
- Scenario 2: Set sensitivity to T1, verify T2/T3 fields dropped
- Scenario 3: Set retention to 0 days for T3, verify tombstoning
- Scenario 4: Roundtrip redacted artifact, verify no data loss in non-redacted fields
- Structured JSON logging: {"test":"redaction","scenario":N,"secrets_found":N,"secrets_redacted":N,"tier":"T1|T2|T3","status":"pass|fail"}

## User Impact
- Pain removed: Operators cannot safely share replay artifacts because they may contain secrets. This bead makes artifacts safe-by-default.
- Measurable outcome: Zero secret leaks in T1 artifacts (verified by automated secret scanning in CI).
- No-regression: Existing recorder_audit.rs A0-A4 tiers unchanged; capture redaction is an additional layer.

Dependencies:
  -> ft-og6q6.2.2 (blocks) - [replay][T1.2] Implement capture extraction from watch/ingest/event bus surfaces
  -> ft-og6q6.2 (parent-child) - [replay][T1] Capture Artifact and Trace Data Plane

Dependents:
  <- ft-og6q6.2.5 (blocks) - [replay][T1.5] Add artifact chunking, compression, hashing, and integrity validation
  <- ft-og6q6.2.7 (blocks) - [replay][T1.7] Build capture integration/e2e test suite and structured logging harness

Comments:

Rebalanced only meta-container priorities (epic + track nodes -> P2) so execution bots/operators focus first on concrete leaf implementation tasks (P1) without losing any scope or capability.\n5. Error running beads viewer: could not open a new TTY: open /dev/tty: device not configured diagnostics show no cycles and no mission-scoped missing-dependency suggestions after revisions.\n\nIntent: keep this graph implementation-ready and self-documenting so future operators can execute directly from beads without external planning docs.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:47:51Z"},{"id":117,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Plan-space optimization pass (2026-02-22, throughput-focused): removed coarse parent-level gates from tracks 5/6/7 and reattached them to the precise integration-certification beads. Changes: (1) track 5 no longer depends directly on C7/D7; E7 now carries those explicit integration gates; (2) track 6 no longer depends directly on E7/C7, relying instead on child-level dependencies (notably F5/F7/F8) that already require integrated evidence; (3) track 7 no longer has blanket deps on F7/E7/D7, with those constraints preserved on specific hardening beads (G1/G4/G5/G6/G7). Also introduced P0 tiering for critical bottleneck beads (A1/A2/A3, B3a/B3/B4, C1/C2, D1) to reduce triage ambiguity for implementation swarms. Outcome after this pass: no cycles, actionable_count increased to 11, blocked_count reduced to 63, and all 74 beads still satisfy unit + e2e + structured-log acceptance contracts.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:50:41Z"},{"id":118,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Plan-space optimization pass (user-focused, no feature loss):\n\n1) Dependency precision improvements to start hardening earlier without weakening final release gates:\n- Rewired G2 from H12 to H11: removed ft-1i2ge.7.2 -> ft-1i2ge.8.12; added ft-1i2ge.7.2 -> ft-1i2ge.8.11.\n- Rewired G4 from H12 to H11: removed ft-1i2ge.7.4 -> ft-1i2ge.8.12; added ft-1i2ge.7.4 -> ft-1i2ge.8.11 (kept ft-1i2ge.7.4 -> ft-1i2ge.6.7).\n\nRationale:\n- G2/G4 are validation tracks that should begin as soon as tx semantics are proven (H11), not wait for rollout-readiness aggregation (H12).\n- This advances fault/performance discovery earlier for operators while preserving final go/no-go strictness (G6/G5 still require H12 and full evidence).\n\n2) Post-change graph diagnostics:\n- br dep cycles --json => count=0 (DAG preserved).\n- bv --robot-insights --label mission => critical-path length reduced from 25 to 24 nodes in top k-path.\n- Mission graph remains phase2_ready with no cycle regressions.\n\n3) Acceptance-contract completeness re-check (all mission beads):\n- unit tests clause: 74/74\n- e2e scripts clause: 74/74\n- structured log clause: 74/74\n- explicit required fields (timestamp/correlation_id/reason_code) present: 74/74\n\nNet effect for users:\n- Earlier, parallelizable hardening work (faster feedback loops, lower late-stage integration risk).\n- No reduction in scope or safety gates; release evidence burden remains intact and self-documenting.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:57:14Z"},{"id":119,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Second optimization pass (post-validation):\n\n- Elevated tx proof stack priorities to P0:\n  - ft-1i2ge.8.10 (tx semantics correctness suite)\n  - ft-1i2ge.8.11 (deterministic tx e2e matrix)\n  Rationale: after dependency rewiring, H11 is the dominant tx bottleneck and now the earliest reliable gate for G2/G4 hardening.\n\n- Updated G2/G4 notes to encode shift-left intent directly in bead metadata:\n  - Validation should begin at H11 (semantic proof), not wait for H12 (rollout aggregation).\n  - H12 remains mandatory downstream via G6/G5 release evidence.\n\n- Re-validated graph and acceptance contract:\n  - DAG integrity: cycles=0\n  - triage: open=74, actionable=11, blocked=63\n  - priorities: P0=11, P1=54, P2=9\n  - acceptance contract coverage (mission subtree): unit=74/74, e2e=74/74, structured-log=74/74, required log fields=74/74\n\nNet user effect: earlier fault/perf feedback against tx semantics, stronger signal that correctness/e2e proof is first-class, and unchanged end-stage safety/release gates.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:57:47Z"},{"id":120,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Third plan-space optimization pass (graph simplification without scope loss):\n\n- Performed a transitive-reduction cleanup of redundant intra-track block edges (30 removals) where dependency reachability was already guaranteed through another direct prerequisite path.\n- No capability, acceptance, or release gates removed; only duplicate edges were eliminated to reduce planning noise and improve operator legibility.\n\nResulting graph quality:\n- DAG preserved: cycles=0\n- Edge count reduced: 184 -> 154 (minus 30 redundant edges)\n- Triage shape stable: open=74, actionable=11, blocked=63\n- Priority distribution preserved: P0=11, P1=54, P2=9\n- Critical-path top length remains 24 nodes (no regression in dependency correctness)\n\nQuality contract validation (all mission beads):\n- unit-test clause: 74/74\n- executable e2e-script clause: 74/74\n- structured-log clause: 74/74\n- required log fields (timestamp/correlation_id/reason_code): 74/74\n\nWhy this helps users:\n- Cleaner dependency graph reduces implementation ambiguity and coordination overhead in swarm execution.\n- Lower edge noise makes true bottlenecks easier to identify and attack early, while preserving every feature and safety gate.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:00:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.1","title":"[mission][track] Domain model and mission lifecycle contract","description":"Define canonical mission entities, lifecycle states, and invariants so all planner/dispatcher components share a single operational contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Mission entities and transitions are explicitly defined with invariants.
2. State transitions include failure/cancel/timeout paths.
3. Contract is machine-checkable via schema/state validation tests.
4. Child tracks cite this contract as source of truth.
5. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests, executable e2e test scripts, and detailed structured logs/artifacts proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
6. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_1.sh and detailed structured logs under tests/e2e/logs/ft_1i2ge_1_*.jsonl proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.

 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.1.1","title":"[mission][A1] Mission nouns, ownership boundaries, and schema pack","description":"Define canonical mission entities: Mission, CandidateAction, Assignment, ReservationIntent, ApprovalState, Outcome, and Escalation.

@@ -0,0 +1,135 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
+LOG_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}/tests/e2e/logs"
+mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"
+
+RUN_ID="$(date +"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")"
+SCENARIO_ID="ft_1i2ge_2_1_readiness_resolver"
+CORRELATION_ID="ft-1i2ge.2.1-${RUN_ID}"
+LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/ft_1i2ge_2_1_${RUN_ID}.jsonl"
+PROBE_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/ft_1i2ge_2_1_${RUN_ID}.probe.log"
+STDOUT_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/ft_1i2ge_2_1_${RUN_ID}.stdout.log"
+
+emit_log() {
+  local outcome="$1"
+  local decision_path="$2"
+  local reason_code="$3"
+  local error_code="$4"
+  local artifact_path="$5"
+  local input_summary="$6"
+  local ts
+  ts="$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")"
+
+  jq -cn \
+    --arg timestamp "${ts}" \
+    --arg component "beads_readiness.e2e" \
+    --arg scenario_id "${SCENARIO_ID}" \
+    --arg correlation_id "${CORRELATION_ID}" \
+    --arg decision_path "${decision_path}" \
+    --arg input_summary "${input_summary}" \
+    --arg outcome "${outcome}" \
+    --arg reason_code "${reason_code}" \
+    --arg error_code "${error_code}" \
+    --arg artifact_path "${artifact_path}" \
+    '{
+      timestamp: $timestamp,
+      component: $component,
+      scenario_id: $scenario_id,
+      correlation_id: $correlation_id,
+      decision_path: $decision_path,
+      input_summary: $input_summary,
+      outcome: $outcome,
+      reason_code: $reason_code,
+      error_code: $error_code,
+      artifact_path: $artifact_path
+    }' >> "${LOG_FILE}"
+}
+
+emit_log \
+  "started" \
+  "script_init" \
+  "none" \
+  "none" \
+  "$(basename "${LOG_FILE}")" \
+  "beads DAG ingestion + readiness resolver validation"
+
+if ! command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  emit_log "failed" "preflight_rch" "rch_missing" "rch_not_found" "$(basename "${LOG_FILE}")" "rch required"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  emit_log "failed" "preflight_jq" "jq_missing" "jq_not_found" "$(basename "${LOG_FILE}")" "jq required"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+set +e
+(
+  cd "${ROOT_DIR}"
+  rch workers probe --all
+) >"${PROBE_FILE}" 2>&1
+probe_status=$?
+set -e
+
+if [[ ${probe_status} -ne 0 ]] || ! grep -q "✓" "${PROBE_FILE}"; then
+  emit_log \
+    "failed" \
+    "preflight_rch_workers" \
+    "rch_workers_unreachable" \
+    "remote_worker_unavailable" \
+    "$(basename "${PROBE_FILE}")" \
+    "No healthy remote RCH worker available"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+TEST_CMDS=(
+  "rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-ft-1i2ge-2-1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core beads_readiness_ -- --nocapture"
+  "rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-ft-1i2ge-2-1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core test_readiness_report_ -- --nocapture"
+)
+
+: >"${STDOUT_FILE}"
+for test_cmd in "${TEST_CMDS[@]}"; do
+  emit_log "running" "cargo_test" "none" "none" "$(basename "${STDOUT_FILE}")" "Executing: ${test_cmd}"
+
+  set +e
+  (
+    cd "${ROOT_DIR}"
+    eval "${test_cmd}"
+  ) 2>&1 | tee -a "${STDOUT_FILE}"
+  status=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
+  set -e
+
+  if grep -q "\[RCH\] local" "${STDOUT_FILE}"; then
+    emit_log "failed" "cargo_test" "rch_fallback_local" "offload_contract_violation" "$(basename "${STDOUT_FILE}")" "rch fell back to local execution"
+    exit 1
+  fi
+
+  if [[ ${status} -ne 0 ]]; then
+    emit_log "failed" "cargo_test" "test_failure" "cargo_test_failed" "$(basename "${STDOUT_FILE}")" "exit=${status}"
+    exit ${status}
+  fi
+
+done
+
+required_markers=(
+  "beads_readiness_resolver_marks_ready_when_blockers_closed ... ok"
+  "beads_readiness_resolver_marks_missing_dependency_as_degraded ... ok"
+  "test_readiness_report_from_details_produces_ready_ids ... ok"
+)
+
+for marker in "${required_markers[@]}"; do
+  if ! grep -q "${marker}" "${STDOUT_FILE}"; then
+    emit_log "failed" "assertion_check" "missing_success_marker" "expected_test_marker_missing" "$(basename "${STDOUT_FILE}")" "Missing marker: ${marker}"
+    exit 1
+  fi
+done
+
+emit_log \
+  "passed" \
+  "resolve_bead_readiness->readiness_report" \
+  "resolver_validated" \
+  "none" \
+  "$(basename "${STDOUT_FILE}")" \
+  "DAG readiness resolver validated with structured hints and degraded-mode codes"
